location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday and Friday may be from home depending on role/team

level_signals: Comp range $211K–$240K base, consistent with Principal IC at a late-stage SaaS company
Expected to influence engineering, design, and executive leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8–10+ years of product management experience, with a focus on growth at B2B SaaS companies.
must_haves.qualifier: focus on growth at B2B SaaS companies

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven track record in driving top-of-funnel acquisition and activation strategies.
Deep experience with AI/ML products, LLM APIs, or third-party platform integrations like ChatGPT or Claude.
Strong analytical foundation with expertise in experimentation, A/B testing, and full-funnel optimization.
Strategic business acumen used to balance user experience with evolving AI-driven revenue models.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

scope.surface: AI Growth — verticalized AI experiences, PLG motions, LLM discovery/acquisition channels, AI Teammates
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, product-led growth with AI consumption revenue
core_responsibilities: Define and deliver verticalized use cases that AI Agents leverage across priority industries, translating workflows into AI-native experiences.
Develop PLG motions that pioneer the 'Copilot-to-Autopilot' journey, enabling autonomous AI Teammates to handle end-to-end workflows.
Manage strategic partnerships with LLM providers to optimize how Asana surfaces in discovery environments, ensuring seamless entry for new users.
Own and deliver on critical Growth KPIs, including AI Net ARR, AI Teammate adoption, and verticalized activation metrics.
Operate with a 0→1 builder mentality to invent new playbooks for AI consumption revenue in a highly ambiguous environment.
Influence cross-functional stakeholders in engineering, design, and executive leadership to align product strategy with emerging technology trends.
Lead Asana's acquisition playbook for emerging LLM-driven channels to drive how new users discover and adopt the platform.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package; form not specified

green_flags: Clear primary metrics named (AI Net ARR, AI Teammate adoption, verticalized activation metrics)
Well-defined product surface with a clear strategic pivot narrative (AI-first organization)
Yearly pay equity audit mentioned — signals pay fairness commitment
Hybrid schedule with specific days named — not vague
red_flags: Role requires presence in Vancouver office (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday minimum) — eliminates remote and US-based candidates
High buzzword density ('Copilot-to-Autopilot', '0→1 builder mentality', 'verticalized') may obscure actual scope
No nice-to-haves section — all requirements presented as hard, leaving little flexibility signaling
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed in JD
Location pay differential noted by company but specific Vancouver vs. other market rates not clarified
